title: 1.1: States that creating a custom alias with an expired signed suffix returns HTTP 412 Precondition Failed with the JSON error {"error":"Alias creation time is expired, please retry"}, since expiry is enforced during signature validation.

1.2: States that creating a custom alias with a tampered signed suffix returns HTTP 412 Precondition Failed with the JSON error {"error":"Alias creation time is expired, please retry"}, since invalid signatures are rejected during validation.
1.3: Reports a WARNING-level server log entry containing "Alias creation time expired for <User ...>" when an expired signed suffix is used, to reflect runtime validation logging.
1.4: Reports a WARNING-level server log entry containing "Alias creation time expired for <User ...>" when a tampered signed suffix is used, to reflect runtime validation logging.
1.6: Reports a DEBUG-level server log entry containing "user <User ...> cannot create any custom alias" when alias quota validation fails, to provide runtime evidence of quota rejection.

1.7: States that no rate-limiting headers appear in alias-creation API responses, including Retry-After, X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, and RateLimit-Policy headers, since rate limiting is enforced without exposing header metadata.
1.9: States that when quota validation passes, no log entry is emitted, since logging only occurs on validation failure.
1.10: States that signed suffix validation is performed by check_suffix_signature located in app/alias_suffix.py.
1.11: States that check_suffix_signature returns None when signature verification fails (both expired and tampered), which triggers alias rejection at runtime.
1.12: States that alias creation is rejected when domain validation fails, returning HTTP 400 with the error {"error":"wrong alias prefix or suffix"}.
1.14: States that quota enforcement via User.can_create_new_alias occurs first in the alias creation execution sequence.
1.15: States that domain validation occurs after signed suffix validation in the alias creation execution sequence.
1.16: States that when all validations pass, alias creation responds with HTTP 200 or HTTP 201, returning the created alias details in the response.
